title: sida carnea
intro: Sida carnea, also known as the prickly sida or country mallow, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to tropical Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, skin infections, and wounds. The medicinal properties of Sida carnea are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. In herbal preparations, Sida carnea is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments to treat a range of health conditions, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential in developing new medications.
